line: GROVE_RESULT: success - added end-to-end ApiHang detection coverage

event.summary: success - added end-to-end ApiHang detection coverage

line: - The ApiHang alert duration is slightly above 120s in practice, so the test should assert `>= 120s` rather than an exact second.

items: The ApiHang alert duration is slightly above 120s in practice, so the test should assert `>= 120s` rather than an exact second.

line: - On this shell, `wait` can print harmless “No record of process” messages for already-reaped background jobs during cleanup.

items: On this shell, `wait` can print harmless “No record of process” messages for already-reaped background jobs during cleanup.

line: - Used a synthetic Claude session fixture under a temporary `HOME` so the e2e test exercises the real `saw watch` CLI without depending on a live Claude session.

items: Used a synthetic Claude session fixture under a temporary `HOME` so the e2e test exercises the real `saw watch` CLI without depending on a live Claude session.

line: - Covered both `--on-stuck warn` and `--on-stuck kill`, plus a non-hanging alive case to ensure no action fires when the process is healthy.

items: Covered both `--on-stuck warn` and `--on-stuck kill`, plus a non-hanging alive case to ensure no action fires when the process is healthy.

line: - For the kill case, the test stops the target with `SIGSTOP`, confirms ApiHang detection, then resumes it with `SIGCONT` so the queued SIGINT can terminate it cleanly while keeping control of cleanup.

items: For the kill case, the test stops the target with `SIGSTOP`, confirms ApiHang detection, then resumes it with `SIGCONT` so the queued SIGINT can terminate it cleanly while keeping control of cleanup.

line: - The test currently emits benign shell cleanup noise (`wait_for: No record of process ...`) even though it passes.
